operating_state: Total revenue decreased 12% sequentially to $9.4 billion in Q3 2022 driven by lower crude oil and NGL prices despite oil and gas production volumes increasing to 1,179 Mboe/d from higher Permian Basin activity. Chemical segment revenue declined on lower PVC prices and reduced volumes while midstream revenue fell due to lower sulfur prices at Al Hosn Gas.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased year-over-year driven by higher surface operations costs in domestic operations and elevated purchased injectant costs in the Permian. Chemical segment faced margin pressure from higher energy and raw material costs despite strong product pricing. Depreciation expenses decreased due to lower per-barrel DD&A rates from higher proved reserves additions.
material_events: Occidental repaid $1.3 billion of debt during the quarter with additional $191 million repaid post-quarter. The company declared $369 million in common dividends and repurchased 41.0 million shares for $2.5 billion during the nine months. A Q1 2022 legal entity reorganization generated a $2.6 billion non-cash tax benefit. Senior Vice President and General Counsel Marcia Backus announced December 2022 departure.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil price volatility with WTI averaging $91.55 in Q3 versus $108.41 in Q2. The company faces continued exposure to geopolitical risks from the Russia-Ukraine war and OPEC production decisions. Inflation pressures impact operational costs though managed through operational efficiencies and longer-term contract pricing.
forward_outlook: Management maintains capital discipline focused on sustaining production levels while maximizing cash flow generation. The company expects to complete its $3.0 billion share repurchase program in Q4 2022 with $562 million remaining. Occidental continues utilizing operating cash flows to reduce financial leverage and execute shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks.
uncertainty_profile: Near-term debt maturities include $362 million in 2023 and $1.3 billion in 2024 with Zero Coupons puttable for $344 million in October 2023. Interest rate swaps face mandatory termination in September 2023. The company maintains financing covenant compliance and expects sufficient liquidity from operations and credit facilities. Environmental remediation liabilities continue under regulatory oversight.
